prompt: Compare average monthly revenue between store regions for 2022 and 2023
Retriever_1_message:
I executed this query to retrieve the required data:

'''SQL
SELECT st.region, s.yr AS year, ROUND(AVG(s.monthly_rev), 2) AS avg_monthly_revenue 
FROM (
    SELECT Store_Number, 
           EXTRACT(YEAR FROM CAST(Sold_Date AS DATE)) AS yr, 
           DATE_TRUNC('month', CAST(Sold_Date AS DATE)) AS month, 
           SUM(Total_Sale_Value) AS monthly_rev 
    FROM sales 
    WHERE EXTRACT(YEAR FROM CAST(Sold_Date AS DATE)) IN (2022, 2023) 
    GROUP BY Store_Number, yr, month
) s 
JOIN stores st ON s.Store_Number = st.Store_Number 
GROUP BY st.region, s.yr 
ORDER BY st.region, s.yr;
'''

The resulting DataFrame has 8 rows with columns : region, year, avg_monthly_revenue
Analyzer_1_message: In 2022, the average monthly revenue by region was as follows: Midwest $10,522.09, Northeast $17,703.52, South $14,925.39, and West $12,241.59. In 2023, the revenues were: Midwest $10,821.44, Northeast $14,970.11, South $14,578.71, and West $12,129.50. Notably, the Northeast saw a decrease in average monthly revenue from 2022 to 2023, while the Midwest and South experienced modest increases.
Visualizer_1_message:
The code for a proper visualization is:

'''python
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

group_col = config['group_by']
x_col = config['x_axis']
y_col = config['y_axis']

groups = sorted(data_df[group_col].unique())
x_labels = sorted(data_df[x_col].unique())
x = np.arange(len(x_labels))
n_series = len(groups)
bar_width = 0.8 / n_series

plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))
for i, group_val in enumerate(groups):
    df_group = data_df[data_df[group_col] == group_val].set_index(x_col)
    y_vals = [df_group.loc[xl, y_col] if xl in df_group.index else 0 for xl in x_labels]
    offset = (i - n_series / 2 + 0.5) * bar_width
    plt.bar(x + offset, y_vals, width=bar_width, label=str(group_val))

plt.xlabel(x_col)
plt.ylabel(y_col)
plt.title(config['title'])
plt.xticks(x, x_labels, rotation=45, ha='right')
plt.legend()
plt.grid(True, axis='y', alpha=0.3)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
'''
 The configuration for matplotlib is:
{'chart_type': 'bar', 'x_axis': 'region', 'y_axis': 'avg_monthly_revenue', 'group_by': 'year', 'title': 'Avg Monthly Revenue by Region: 2022 vs 2023'}

    CASE 
        WHEN s.On_Promo = 1 THEN 'Promo'

prompt: Return the top 3 brands by realized average selling price in 2023, where average price is computed as total revenue divided by total units sold, along with total units sold per brand
Retriever_1_message:
I executed this query to retrieve the required data:

'''SQL
SELECT p.brand, 
       ROUND(SUM(s.Total_Sale_Value) / NULLIF(SUM(s.Qty_Sold), 0), 2) AS realized_avg_selling_price, 
       SUM(s.Qty_Sold) AS total_units_sold 
FROM sales s 
JOIN products p ON s.SKU_Coded = p.SKU_Coded 
WHERE EXTRACT(YEAR FROM s.Sold_Date) = 2023 
GROUP BY p.brand 
ORDER BY realized_avg_selling_price DESC 
LIMIT 3;

       p.is_organic, 
       SUM(s.Total_Sale_Value) AS Total_Revenue 
FROM sales s 
JOIN products p ON s.SKU_Coded = p.SKU_Coded 
JOIN stores st ON s.Store_Number = st.Store_Number 
WHERE EXTRACT(YEAR FROM s.Sold_Date) = 2023 
GROUP BY st.region, p.is_organic 
ORDER BY st.region, p.is_organic;
'''

The resulting DataFrame has 8 rows with columns : region, is_organic, Total_Revenue

The Northeast produced revenues of $604,879.67 from Non-Organic and $113,685.46 from Organic. The South saw Non-Organic bring in $1,560,531.43 and Organic $320,121.83, while the West's Non-Organic sales reached $1,701,793.25, compared to $335,963.54 from Organic products.

prompt: Compare average monthly revenue between store types for 2022 and 2023
Retriever_1_message:
I executed this query to retrieve the required data:

'''SQL
SELECT st.store_type, s.yr AS year, ROUND(AVG(s.monthly_rev), 2) AS avg_monthly_revenue 
FROM (
    SELECT Store_Number, 
           EXTRACT(YEAR FROM CAST(Sold_Date AS DATE)) AS yr, 
           DATE_TRUNC('month', CAST(Sold_Date AS DATE)) AS month, 
           SUM(Total_Sale_Value) AS monthly_rev 
    FROM sales 
    WHERE EXTRACT(YEAR FROM CAST(Sold_Date AS DATE)) IN (2022, 2023) 
    GROUP BY Store_Number, yr, month
) s 
JOIN stores st ON s.Store_Number = st.Store_Number 
GROUP BY st.store_type, s.yr 
ORDER BY st.store_type, s.yr;
'''

The resulting DataFrame has 8 rows with columns : store_type, year, avg_monthly_revenue
Analyzer_1_message: In 2022, the average monthly revenue for Convenience stores was $15,914.67, which decreased to $14,881.74 in 2023. Discount stores saw a minor decrease from $13,206.19 in 2022 to $13,122.83 in 2023, while Hypermarkets dropped from $12,657.14 to $12,383.27, and Supermarkets from $12,829.11 to $12,668.38 over the same period. Overall, there is a trend of declining average monthly revenues across all store types from 2022 to 2023.

prompt: Show promo revenue share by product category for 2023 as a bar chart, expressing the share as a percentage of total revenue
Retriever_1_message:
I executed this query to retrieve the required data:

'''SQL
SELECT p.category, 
       ROUND(SUM(CASE WHEN s.On_Promo = 1 THEN s.Total_Sale_Value ELSE 0 END) / NULLIF(SUM(s.Total_Sale_Value), 0) * 100, 2) AS promo_revenue_share_percentage
FROM sales s 
JOIN products p ON s.SKU_Coded = p.SKU_Coded 
WHERE EXTRACT(YEAR FROM s.Sold_Date) = 2023 
GROUP BY p.category 
ORDER BY promo_revenue_share_percentage DESC;
'''

The resulting DataFrame has 6 rows with columns : category, promo_revenue_share_percentage
Analyzer_1_message: In 2023, the promotional revenue share by product category is as follows: Snacks had the highest share at 6.26%, followed by Dairy at 5.0%, Personal Care at 1.9%, and Bakery at 0.21%. The categories of Household and Beverages reported no promotional revenue share.

prompt: Return the top 10 region-category combinations by year-over-year revenue growth from 2022 to 2023, showing revenue for both years and the growth expressed as a percentage rounded to 2 decimal places
Retriever_1_message:
I executed this query to retrieve the required data:

'''SQL
SELECT 
    st.region, 
    p.category, 
    SUM(CASE WHEN YEAR(s.Sold_Date) = 2022 THEN s.Total_Sale_Value ELSE 0 END) AS revenue_2022,
    SUM(CASE WHEN YEAR(s.Sold_Date) = 2023 THEN s.Total_Sale_Value ELSE 0 END) AS revenue_2023,
    ROUND(
        (SUM(CASE WHEN YEAR(s.Sold_Date) = 2023 THEN s.Total_Sale_Value ELSE 0 END) - 
         SUM(CASE WHEN YEAR(s.Sold_Date) = 2022 THEN s.Total_Sale_Value ELSE 0 END)) / 
         NULLIF(SUM(CASE WHEN YEAR(s.Sold_Date) = 2022 THEN s.Total_Sale_Value ELSE 0 END), 0) * 100, 
        2) AS revenue_growth_percentage
FROM 
    sales s 
JOIN 
    products p ON s.SKU_Coded = p.SKU_Coded 
JOIN 
    stores st ON s.Store_Number = st.Store_Number 
WHERE 
    YEAR(s.Sold_Date) IN (2022, 2023) 
GROUP BY 
    st.region, p.category 
ORDER BY 
    revenue_growth_percentage DESC 
LIMIT 10;
'''

The resulting DataFrame has 10 rows with columns : region, category, revenue_2022, revenue_2023, revenue_growth_percentage
